name: Afghanistan
facts: Lapis lazuli has been mined here for over 6,000 years
Buzkashi uses a goat carcass — the thrilling national sport
Home to the Hindu Kush, one of the world's highest mountain ranges
Produces over 80% of the world's illicit opium
The giant Buddhas of Bamiyan were carved in the 6th century AD

name: Albania
facts: Nodding means 'no' and shaking means 'yes' — the exact opposite!
Has 750,000 bunkers — one for every 4 citizens
The double-headed eagle on the flag dates back 600 years
Mother Teresa was of Albanian heritage
Blood feuds under the Kanun code occasionally persist today

name: Algeria
facts: Largest country in Africa by area
Sahara Desert covers over 80% of the territory
Home to Timgad — the 'Pompeii of Africa'
Algeria has the largest military in Africa
Tuareg people navigate the Sahara by century-old star maps

name: Argentina
facts: Tango was born in Buenos Aires in the 1880s
Largest Spanish-speaking country in the world by area
Patagonia holds the third-largest freshwater reserve on Earth
First dinosaur fossil was found here in 1882
Has more psychoanalysts per capita than any other country

name: Australia
facts: The only country that is also a continent
Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest living structure
Kangaroos outnumber humans 2 to 1
21 of the world's 25 most venomous snakes live here
The platypus is one of only five egg-laying mammals on Earth

name: Brazil
facts: Contains 60% of the Amazon rainforest
More primate species here than anywhere else on Earth
Only Portuguese-speaking country in the Americas
The Amazon discharges 20% of all freshwater entering the oceans
Carnival in Rio attracts over 2 million people daily

name: Canada
facts: Longest coastline in the world at 202,080 km
Holds 20% of the world's freshwater supply
Has more lakes than the rest of the world combined
Hockey was invented in Canada in the 1800s
The CN Tower was the world's tallest structure for 34 years

name: China
facts: The Great Wall stretches over 21,000 km
China invented paper, printing, gunpowder, and the compass
Giant pandas are only found wild in Sichuan province
Uses only one time zone despite spanning five geographical ones
The Forbidden City has 9,999 rooms — one short of heavenly perfection

name: Colombia
facts: Only South American country with both Pacific and Caribbean coasts
World's largest producer of emeralds
More bird species here than any other country on Earth
Gabriel García Márquez, inventor of magical realism, was Colombian
The name Colombia derives from Christopher Columbus

name: Egypt
facts: The Great Pyramid is the only surviving Wonder of the Ancient World
Ancient Egyptians invented the 365-day calendar
Home to the Nile — the world's longest river
Cairo is the largest city in Africa and the Arab world
Ancient Egyptians used over 700 hieroglyphic symbols

name: Ethiopia
facts: Oldest independent country in Africa
Coffee was discovered here — the word comes from the 'Kaffa' region
Has its own calendar with 13 months
Addis Ababa is the highest capital city in Africa
Lucy, one of humanity's oldest ancestors (3.2M years), was found here

name: France
facts: Most visited country on Earth with 90 million tourists per year
Has 246 types of cheese — one for every day
The Eiffel Tower grows 6 inches taller in summer from heat expansion
French was the world's diplomatic language until the 20th century
The Louvre is the world's most visited art museum

name: Germany
facts: Germany has 1,500 different types of beer
The printing press was invented by Gutenberg in Germany in 1440
Has the 4th largest economy in the world
There are over 20,000 castles across Germany
The first magazine in history launched in Germany in 1663

name: Ghana
facts: First sub-Saharan country to gain independence in 1957
One of the world's top gold producers
Lake Volta is the world's largest artificial lake by area
Coffin art is a celebrated tradition — planes, fish, and phones shaped coffins
Home to the legendary and powerful Ashanti Kingdom

name: Greece
facts: Has over 6,000 islands but only 227 are inhabited
The Olympics were born in ancient Greece in 776 BC
Longest coastline in the Mediterranean Sea
More archaeological museums per capita than any other country
The Greek alphabet is the ancestor of Latin and Cyrillic scripts

name: India
facts: World's largest democracy with 1.4 billion people
Chess was invented in India around the 6th century AD
Has 22 official languages and hundreds of dialects
The Taj Mahal took 22 years and 22,000 workers to build
Produces more films than any other country on Earth

name: Indonesia
facts: World's largest archipelago with over 17,000 islands
Has the highest marine biodiversity on Earth
Komodo dragons, the world's largest lizards, live only here
The 1883 Krakatoa eruption was heard 4,800 km away
Has more active volcanoes than any other country

name: Iran
facts: One of the world's oldest civilizations at over 7,000 years
World's largest producer of saffron
Persian poetry is considered among the finest ever written
Sits on 10% of the world's proven oil reserves
Invented the concept of the postal system in 550 BC

name: Italy
facts: Has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country (58)
Invented pizza, pasta, and espresso
The Vatican inside Rome is the world's smallest country
Ancient Rome gave the world law, roads, and aqueducts
Italian is the closest living language to classical Latin

name: Japan
facts: Has the world's oldest company — Kongō Gumi, founded 578 AD
Experiences about 1,500 earthquakes every year
More vending machines per person than any other country
Japan's rail system has an average delay of just 54 seconds
Mount Fuji is climbed by over 300,000 people annually

name: Kenya
facts: Home to the world's largest annual wildlife migration
Nairobi is one of only 3 cities with a national park inside city limits
Kenya produces world-class marathon runners due to high-altitude training
The Great Rift Valley runs through Kenya like a geological spine
The Maasai people measure wealth in cattle and children

name: Mexico
facts: Birthplace of chocolate, corn, and avocados
Mexico City sinks 20 cm per year — built on a drained lake
The eagle-and-snake flag image comes from an Aztec legend
Mexico has 35 UNESCO World Heritage Sites
The Aztec capital Tenochtitlan was built on a lake island

name: Morocco
facts: Home to the world's oldest university — al-Qarawiyyin, founded 859 AD
One of only 3 countries with both Atlantic and Mediterranean coasts
Argan oil, the world's rarest, comes exclusively from Morocco
Traditional zellige tilework dates back to the 10th century
Morocco was among the first nations to recognize US independence

name: Netherlands
facts: One-third of the Netherlands is below sea level
Amsterdam has more bicycles than people
Has more canals than Venice
The Dutch Golden Age (17th century) made them the richest nation
Tulips are not native to the Netherlands — they came from Central Asia

name: New Zealand
facts: First country to give women the right to vote in 1893
Māori name Aotearoa means 'Land of the Long White Cloud'
Lord of the Rings was filmed entirely in New Zealand
Has more sheep per person than almost any other country
New Zealand has the world's highest proportion of endangered species

name: Nigeria
facts: Most populous country in Africa with 206 million people
Nollywood produces more films per year than Hollywood
Has over 500 distinct ethnic groups and languages
Lagos is Africa's largest city with over 20 million people
Nigeria is Africa's largest economy by GDP

name: Norway
facts: Has the world's longest road tunnel at 24.5 km
Discovered oil in 1969, transforming from poor fishing nation to one of richest
Some Norwegians experience 76 days of continuous midnight sun
Invented the paper clip and the cheese slicer
Norway's sovereign wealth fund is the world's largest at over $1.3 trillion

name: Peru
facts: Machu Picchu sits at 2,430 meters above sea level
Home to the Inca Empire, the largest in pre-Columbian America
The Amazon River begins in the Peruvian Andes
Peru has 90 microclimates — more than almost any country
Potatoes were first cultivated in Peru around 8,000 years ago

name: Russia
facts: Largest country on Earth spanning 11 time zones
Lake Baikal holds 20% of the world's unfrozen surface freshwater
Trans-Siberian Railway is the world's longest at 9,288 km
Moscow's metro is famous for its ornate, palace-like stations
Russia has 23 UNESCO World Heritage Sites

name: Saudi Arabia
facts: Home to Mecca, Islam's holiest city, visited by 2+ million yearly
Has 25% of the world's known oil reserves
The Empty Quarter is the world's largest continuous sand desert
Has no rivers — only dry seasonal wadis
Women were only allowed to drive from June 2018

name: South Africa
facts: Has 3 capital cities — one per branch of government
Produces 75% of the world's platinum
Has 11 official languages
The Cradle of Humankind has some of the oldest hominid fossils
Nelson Mandela was imprisoned for 27 years before becoming president

name: South Korea
facts: Has the world's fastest average internet speeds
K-pop and K-drama are now global cultural phenomena
Koreans consider themselves 1 year old at birth
Seoul is one of the world's 10 largest cities
Went from war-torn poverty to top-20 economy in just 50 years

name: Spain
facts: Has 50 UNESCO World Heritage Sites
La Sagrada Família has been under construction since 1882
Spanish is the 2nd most spoken language by native speakers
Spain invented the mop, submarine, and stapler
La Tomatina: 40,000 kg of tomatoes hurled in the world's largest food fight

name: Sweden
facts: Invented the safety match, zipper, and Bluetooth
Has a 'right to roam' — you can legally camp anywhere in nature
ABBA, IKEA, Spotify, and Minecraft all come from Sweden
Sweden has more than 220,000 islands
Recycles 99% of all household waste

name: Switzerland
facts: Has not been at war since 1815
The World Wide Web was invented at CERN in Switzerland
Invented milk chocolate and the Swiss Army Knife
Has 4 official languages: German, French, Italian, Romansh
Bern means 'bears' — there are still real bears kept in the city

name: Thailand
facts: Never colonized by a European power — the only SE Asian nation
World's largest exporter of rice and natural rubber
Bangkok's full ceremonial name is 168 characters long
Has over 40,000 Buddhist temples
The number 9 is very lucky — the Thai word sounds like 'progress'

name: Turkey
facts: Istanbul is the only city in the world spanning two continents
Turkey introduced coffee to Europe via the Ottoman Empire
The first wheat and barley were planted here 10,000 years ago
Has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than all of South America
Santa Claus (St. Nicholas) was born in what is now Turkey

name: Ukraine
facts: Largest country entirely within Europe
Kyiv's Arsenalna metro station is the world's deepest at 105.5 m
The helicopter was invented by Ukrainian Igor Sikorsky in 1939
Ukraine is the 'breadbasket of Europe' — produces 25% of global grain
The Chernobyl Exclusion Zone is now a thriving wildlife sanctuary

name: United Kingdom
facts: Invented the World Wide Web, steam engine, and telephone
The British Empire once covered a quarter of the Earth's surface
London was the world's largest city from 1831 to 1925
Shakespeare invented over 1,700 words we still use today
UK has no written constitution — one of only 3 countries without one

name: United States
facts: Has the world's largest economy
Americans eat the equivalent of 100 acres of pizza per day
Has won more Nobel Prizes than any other country
The Library of Congress is the world's largest library
50 stars represent the 50 states — originally there were only 13

name: Vietnam
facts: World's largest exporter of cashews
Ha Long Bay has 2,000+ limestone islands rising from emerald waters
Son Doong is the world's largest cave with its own weather system
2nd largest coffee producer in the world after Brazil
One of the few remaining communist-governed countries
